channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Apply for the Day of the African Child 2026 Digital Youth Media Fellowship: 10 Creators Wanted to Amplify WASH Voices Across Africa
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The Day of the African Child 2026 Digital Campaign is a powerful regional youth initiative designed to elevate the voices, creativity, and lived experiences of young people across West and Central Africa. Led by Plan International West and Central Africa, this opportunity brings together young digital storytellers, activists, and content creators to highlight critical issues […]

The post Apply for the Day of the African Child 2026 Digital Youth Media Fellowship: 10 Creators Wanted to Amplify WASH Voices Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Day of the African Child 2026 Digital Campaign is a powerful regional youth initiative designed to elevate the voices, creativity, and lived experiences of young people across West and Central Africa. Led by Plan International West and Central Africa, this opportunity brings together young digital storytellers, activists, and content creators to highlight critical issues affecting children and communities.

Centered on the theme of the Day of the African Child 2026, this campaign focuses on one of the most urgent development priorities in Africa: universal access to Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene (WASH).

Selected participants will join a regional storytelling movement that uses mobile journalism, short-form video content, and digital advocacy to spark awareness and inspire action across communities.

About the Opportunity

This initiative is a regional digital storytelling program seeking 10 young activists and content creators who are passionate about social impact and skilled (or willing to learn) in mobile-based content production.

The goal is to empower youth to document and share real stories about:

  • Access to clean water
  • Hygiene practices in communities
  • Sanitation challenges affecting children and families
  • Youth-led solutions to WASH issues

Participants will be trained, mentored, and supported to produce impactful digital content that will be showcased across regional platforms.

What Selected Participants Will Receive

This program offers a comprehensive creative and professional development experience, including:

1. Free Mobile Journalism Training

Participants will receive hands-on training in:

  • Mobile storytelling techniques
  • Smartphone video production
  • Ethical reporting and storytelling
  • Editing and publishing content for social media

2. Digital Content Creation Support

Participants will learn how to produce:

  • Reels and short-form videos
  • POV storytelling content
  • Advocacy-driven digital narratives
  • Social media campaigns with impact

3. Regional Visibility

Selected creators will gain:

  • Exposure across regional digital platforms
  • Recognition within youth and development networks
  • Opportunities to contribute to a large-scale awareness campaign

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Be young activists or digital content creators
  • Be active on social media or willing to engage in digital storytelling
  • Have interest in advocacy, especially around WASH issues
  • Be available for training and campaign participation

Application Process

Interested applicants must complete a short online form, which takes approximately 5 to 7 minutes.

The form collects:

  • Personal information
  • Country and city of residence
  • Contact details (email and WhatsApp)
  • Basic demographic data

Participating Countries

Applicants must be from one of the following countries:

  • Benin, Burkina Faso, Cameroon, Central African Republic, Chad
  • Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, Liberia, Mali
  • Niger, Nigeria, Senegal, Sierra Leone, Togo

Important Deadline

Application Deadline: 18 May 2026

Late submissions will not be considered, so early application is strongly encouraged.

How to Apply

Submit your application using the official form below:

Apply Here

Why This Opportunity Matters

Water, sanitation, and hygiene remain critical challenges affecting millions of children across Africa. Through this program, young creators will not only gain skills but also become powerful voices driving awareness and change.

By participating, you will:

  • Strengthen your storytelling and digital media skills
  • Join a continental youth advocacy movement
  • Contribute to real-world impact on WASH issues
  • Build your visibility as a digital creator

For more global fellowship opportunities Click here

The post Apply for the Day of the African Child 2026 Digital Youth Media Fellowship: 10 Creators Wanted to Amplify WASH Voices Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Aspire Leaders Program 2026 Cohort 3 Applications Now Open: Global Free Online Leadership Development Opportunity for Young Changemakers
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The 2026 Aspire Leaders Program – Cohort 3 is now officially open for applications, offering a powerful opportunity for young, ambitious individuals from around the world to develop leadership skills, build global networks, and gain career-enhancing mentorship. This fully online and free program is designed to support emerging leaders, especially those from limited-income backgrounds or […]

The post Aspire Leaders Program 2026 Cohort 3 Applications Now Open: Global Free Online Leadership Development Opportunity for Young Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The 2026 Aspire Leaders Program – Cohort 3 is now officially open for applications, offering a powerful opportunity for young, ambitious individuals from around the world to develop leadership skills, build global networks, and gain career-enhancing mentorship. This fully online and free program is designed to support emerging leaders, especially those from limited-income backgrounds or who are first-generation university students.

Hosted by the Aspire Institute Inc, the program provides a structured leadership journey combining personal development, professional training, and real-world problem-solving experience. Cohort 3 will run from July 22, 2026 to September 20, 2026, with applications closing on July 10, 2026.

Overview of the Aspire Leaders Program

The Aspire Leaders Program is a globally accessible leadership development initiative that brings together students and recent graduates from diverse backgrounds.

It is designed to be flexible, meaning participants can engage in the program alongside their academic studies or work commitments. The entire program is delivered in English and emphasizes practical leadership skills, global collaboration, and social impact.

Participants join a global cohort of peers and mentors, creating a dynamic environment for learning, networking, and growth.

Program Timeline for Cohort 3 (2026)

The Cohort 3 program is structured over approximately two months:

  • Program Start Date: July 22, 2026
  • Program End Date: September 20, 2026
  • Application Deadline: July 10, 2026

This timeline ensures participants have sufficient time to engage in structured learning modules while balancing other responsibilities.

Who Can Apply

The Aspire Leaders Program is highly inclusive and designed to support underrepresented and emerging leaders globally.

Applicants must meet the following eligibility criteria:

  • Age between 18 and 29 years old
  • From a limited-income background OR first-generation university student
  • Currently enrolled in a university OR recently graduated

Importantly, applicants who meet the criteria and complete a short application (approximately 10 minutes) are typically accepted into the program.

Structure of the Aspire Leaders Program Journey

The program is divided into two main learning phases, supported by masterclasses and live interactive sessions.

Weeks 1–4: Foundations of Leadership

During the first phase, participants focus on personal leadership development and foundational skills.

Key learning components include:

  • Global peer-to-peer live interactions
  • Strengths-based leadership assessment
  • Identifying and analyzing social issues
  • Developing self-awareness as a leader
  • Setting SMART goals for personal and professional growth

This phase helps participants understand their leadership identity and begin building core competencies.

Weeks 5–9: Digital Transformation, AI, and Community Action

The second phase shifts focus toward practical application and global impact.

Key areas include:

  • Digital transformation and artificial intelligence
  • Solving real-world community challenges
  • Designing and implementing community-based initiatives
  • Building trust and collaborative leadership strategies
  • Professional development training co-created with Harvard Business School, including:
    • Resume development
    • Interview preparation
    • Cover letter writing
    • Career planning and exploration

This phase ensures participants not only learn leadership theory but also apply it in meaningful real-world contexts.

Masterclasses (Throughout the Program)

Participants also benefit from ongoing masterclasses delivered by global educators.

These include:

  • Live interactive sessions with international experts
  • Real-time discussions with peers and mentors
  • Exposure to global leadership perspectives
  • Immersive learning experiences across disciplines

These masterclasses enrich the learning journey and provide direct access to world-class knowledge.

Extended Leadership Opportunities

After completing the program, participants join a growing global alumni network that offers:

  • Continued mentorship opportunities
  • Access to funding and leadership initiatives
  • Networking with global changemakers
  • Career development support
  • Long-term community engagement

This ensures the program’s impact extends far beyond the initial 9-week learning journey.

Why This Program Matters

The Aspire Leaders Program is more than just training—it is a global leadership platform designed to empower the next generation of changemakers.

Key benefits include:

  • Free access to high-quality leadership training
  • Global networking with peers and mentors
  • Career readiness support and professional development tools
  • Exposure to digital transformation and AI concepts
  • Real-world community impact experience
  • Strong emphasis on inclusion and equity in leadership development

Application Process

Applicants are required to complete a short online application form, taking approximately 10 minutes.

Click Here To apply 

The 2026 Aspire Leaders Program Cohort 3 presents a rare opportunity for young leaders to gain global exposure, develop essential leadership skills, and connect with a diverse international community. With its strong focus on accessibility, professional development, and social impact, the program continues to empower the next generation of global changemakers.

Click Here for More information about this opportunity

Interested applicants are encouraged to apply early before the July 10, 2026 deadline.

 

For more global fellowship opportunities Click here.

 

The post Aspire Leaders Program 2026 Cohort 3 Applications Now Open: Global Free Online Leadership Development Opportunity for Young Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: KNUST Postdoctoral Research Position 2026: Political Parties in Africa and Democracy (PPADEM) Project
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for a Postdoctoral Research Associate position at the Kwame Nkrumah University of Science and Technology (KNUST) in Kumasi, Ghana. Hosted by the Department of History, Political Science and Public Administration, this two-year postdoctoral opportunity is part of the prestigious Political Parties in Africa and Democracy (PPADEM) project, funded by the European […]

The post KNUST Postdoctoral Research Position 2026: Political Parties in Africa and Democracy (PPADEM) Project appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for a Postdoctoral Research Associate position at the Kwame Nkrumah University of Science and Technology (KNUST) in Kumasi, Ghana.

Hosted by the Department of History, Political Science and Public Administration, this two-year postdoctoral opportunity is part of the prestigious Political Parties in Africa and Democracy (PPADEM) project, funded by the European Research Council (ERC).

The project is an international collaboration involving KNUST, the University of Strathclyde, the University of Bristol, and the University of Reading.

This is an outstanding opportunity for emerging scholars in political science and related fields to contribute to one of the largest empirical studies of political parties ever undertaken globally.

About the PPADEM Project

The Political Parties in Africa and Democracy (PPADEM) project seeks to understand the role political parties play in democratic progress and decline across Africa.

The research addresses three core questions:

  1. What role do political parties play in democratization in Africa?
  2. Under what conditions do they support or undermine democracy?
  3. How can political parties be strengthened to help counter democratic decline on the continent?

Position Overview

The successful candidate will join an international team of senior and early-career scholars and will play a leading role in:

  • Survey design and research instrument development
  • Data collection and management
  • Cross-national dataset construction and analysis
  • Academic publishing
  • Conference presentations
  • Stakeholder engagement and dissemination
  • Limited undergraduate and postgraduate teaching at KNUST

Contract Details

  • Position: Postdoctoral Research Associate
  • Duration: Two years (subject to annual performance evaluation)
  • Start Date: July/August 2026 or as soon as possible thereafter
  • Location: KNUST, Kumasi, Ghana

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Hold a PhD in Political Science or a closely related social science discipline by the time of appointment
  • Not be employed full-time at KNUST or any other public university/institution
  • Demonstrate expertise in survey design and survey data analysis
  • Have a strong background in comparative politics

Desirable Qualifications

Preference will be given to candidates with:

  • Publications appropriate to their career stage
  • Research coordination and management experience
  • Expertise in elections, public opinion, party politics, or African politics
  • Qualitative research experience (archival and interview methods)
  • Strong organizational and leadership skills
  • Excellent written and spoken English

Application Materials

Applicants must submit a single PDF file containing:

  • Cover letter (maximum two pages)
  • Curriculum Vitae
  • Two-page research plan aligned with the PPADEM project
  • Writing sample (working paper or published article/chapter)
  • Two recommendation letters

Important Note on AI Use

KNUST has stated that applications relying heavily on AI-generated content may appear generic and are unlikely to advance to the interview stage. Applicants are encouraged to ensure that all written materials reflect their authentic voice and specific experience.

Application Deadline

Applications must be submitted by:

1 June 2026

How to Apply

Send your application as a single PDF to:

ppadem@knust.edu.gh

For project-related inquiries, contact:

Prof. George Bob-Milliar
gbobmilliar.cass@knust.edu.gh

For additional information, visit here.

For more Fellowship Opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post KNUST Postdoctoral Research Position 2026: Political Parties in Africa and Democracy (PPADEM) Project appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Netherlands-Financed IOM JPO Programme Officer (Returns) Vacancy in Syria 2026: Fully Funded UN Career Opportunity for Young Professionals
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
Young professionals seeking to launch a global career within the United Nations system now have an exciting opportunity to join the International Organization for Migration (IOM) through the Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme financed by the Netherlands. The newly announced Programme Officer (Returns) position is based in Damascus, Syria, and is being administered through the […]

The post Netherlands-Financed IOM JPO Programme Officer (Returns) Vacancy in Syria 2026: Fully Funded UN Career Opportunity for Young Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young professionals seeking to launch a global career within the United Nations system now have an exciting opportunity to join the International Organization for Migration (IOM) through the Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme financed by the Netherlands.

The newly announced Programme Officer (Returns) position is based in Damascus, Syria, and is being administered through the Nedworc Foundation under the Dutch JPO Programme. This prestigious opportunity is specifically designed for talented young professionals who are passionate about humanitarian work, international development, migration management, and multilateral cooperation.

The vacancy is open to candidates from eligible countries listed by the Nedworc Foundation, giving qualified young professionals the chance to gain hands-on experience within one of the world’s leading international organizations.

What is the IOM JPO Programme?

The Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme is a highly competitive initiative that allows young professionals to work within United Nations organizations while gaining valuable international experience.

Through this programme, selected candidates are placed in strategic roles where they contribute directly to global humanitarian and development efforts. The programme is financed by donor governments, and in this case, the Government of the Netherlands is sponsoring the opportunity through the Nedworc Foundation.

Working as a JPO provides participants with:

  • International exposure within the UN system
  • Professional development opportunities
  • Practical field experience
  • Networking opportunities with global experts
  • A pathway toward long-term international careers
  • Experience in humanitarian operations and migration governance

For many professionals, a JPO placement serves as a stepping stone into permanent careers within the United Nations and related international organizations.

About the Programme Officer (Returns) Position

The selected candidate will serve as a Programme Officer (Returns) with IOM in Damascus, Syria.

The role focuses on supporting voluntary return operations and helping ensure that migration return processes are conducted safely, humanely, and effectively.

Under the supervision of the Chief of Mission and the Senior Programmes Coordinator/Head of Programmes, the successful candidate will work closely with project managers and colleagues across protection, health, and resettlement sectors.

Key Responsibilities

The Programme Officer will contribute to several operational and coordination activities, including:

  1. Supporting the planning and coordination of voluntary return movements
  2. Assisting in the operational setup for return programmes
  3. Supporting the establishment of operational infrastructure
  4. Coordinating safe transportation arrangements for returnees
  5. Implementing protection-sensitive approaches throughout return operations
  6. Liaising with stakeholders and humanitarian partners
  7. Supporting returnees during different phases of their journey
  8. Collaborating with regional and global IOM teams

The role requires strong organizational skills, coordination abilities, and an understanding of humanitarian and migration-related operations.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This JPO position offers far more than a regular job opportunity. It provides early-career professionals with the chance to directly contribute to international humanitarian efforts in a highly strategic environment.

Successful applicants will:

  • Gain experience within a UN agency
  • Build expertise in migration and humanitarian programming
  • Develop international leadership skills
  • Work in a multicultural environment
  • Strengthen policy and operational knowledge
  • Expand global professional networks
  • Contribute to meaningful humanitarian impact

For aspiring international civil servants, this opportunity represents a major career milestone.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates interested in this opportunity must meet strict eligibility requirements established by the Nedworc Foundation and the Dutch JPO Programme.

Nationality Requirement

Applicants must hold the nationality of one of the eligible countries listed by the Nedworc Foundation.

Additionally:

  • Substantive parts of the candidate’s Bachelor’s and/or Master’s education must have taken place in one of the eligible countries.

Age Requirement

Applicants must be:

  • A maximum of 32 years old as of 31 December of the application year.

Educational Requirements

Applicants must meet one of the following academic qualification pathways:

Option 1

  • A Master’s degree or equivalent in:
    • International Relations
    • Political Science
    • Humanitarian Affairs
    • Economic Development
    • Business Administration
    • Public Administration
    • International Studies
    • Or a related field

AND

  • At least two years of relevant professional experience.

Option 2

  • A Bachelor’s degree from an accredited academic institution

AND

  • At least four years of relevant professional experience.

Skills and Professional Background Expected

Ideal candidates are likely to possess:

  • Knowledge of migration and humanitarian issues
  • Strong communication and coordination skills
  • Experience in international development or humanitarian operations
  • Ability to work in multicultural and high-pressure environments
  • Stakeholder engagement and partnership-building skills
  • Operational planning capabilities
  • Strong analytical and reporting skills

Location of the Assignment

The position is based in Damascus, Syria.

Working in Syria provides exposure to complex humanitarian operations and migration-related challenges, making it an especially valuable professional experience for candidates seeking careers in international humanitarian response and development.

Application Deadline

The application deadline for this opportunity is:

4 June 2026

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early and carefully review all eligibility requirements before submitting their applications.

Important Application Notes

Before applying, candidates should:

  • Carefully review the official selection criteria
  • Read the FAQ section on the Nedworc Foundation website
  • Confirm that their nationality appears on the eligible countries list
  • Verify that they meet the age requirement
  • Ensure their academic qualifications satisfy programme requirements
  • Prepare all required supporting documents in advance

Failure to meet the eligibility conditions may result in disqualification.

How to Apply

Click here to apply

Applicants should thoroughly read the full job description before applying.

Click here for more information about this job opportunity

The Netherlands-financed IOM JPO Programme Officer (Returns) vacancy presents a remarkable opportunity for young professionals seeking to begin impactful careers within the United Nations system.

With direct involvement in humanitarian operations, migration programming, and international coordination, the role offers invaluable professional growth while contributing to meaningful global initiatives.

Qualified candidates who meet the nationality, education, and age requirements should strongly consider applying before the deadline.

For more global job opportunities Click here

The post Netherlands-Financed IOM JPO Programme Officer (Returns) Vacancy in Syria 2026: Fully Funded UN Career Opportunity for Young Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Riley’s Way Youth Leadership Retreat 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Weekend for High School Students Aged 15–18
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Riley’s Way Youth Leadership Retreat 2026, a transformative leadership experience for high school students who are passionate about creating positive change in their communities. Organized by Riley’s Way Foundation, this inspiring retreat brings together more than 120 students from across the United States for a weekend of connection, leadership […]

The post Riley’s Way Youth Leadership Retreat 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Weekend for High School Students Aged 15–18 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Riley’s Way Youth Leadership Retreat 2026, a transformative leadership experience for high school students who are passionate about creating positive change in their communities.

Organized by Riley’s Way Foundation, this inspiring retreat brings together more than 120 students from across the United States for a weekend of connection, leadership development, and community building.

The program is designed for young people who believe in leading with kindness, empathy, and purpose. No prior leadership experience is required.

About the Youth Leadership Retreat

The Riley’s Way Youth Leadership Retreat is an immersive weekend experience where students learn how to turn their passions into meaningful action.

Participants will engage in workshops, discussions, and collaborative activities that help them build confidence, strengthen leadership skills, and connect with peers who are committed to making the world better.

Program Benefits

Selected participants will receive:

  • Access to a transformative weekend leadership retreat
  • Leadership and personal development training
  • Opportunities to connect with more than 120 like-minded students
  • Guidance on how to create change in their communities
  • A supportive network focused on kindness and empathy

Who Should Apply

This opportunity is ideal for students who:

  • Are between 15 and 18 years old
  • Are currently in high school
  • Care deeply about issues they want to address
  • Want to create positive change in their communities
  • Are interested in leadership, even without prior experience

Why Apply?

The retreat offers a unique opportunity to:

  • Discover your leadership potential
  • Build lasting friendships
  • Learn practical strategies for social impact
  • Gain confidence in your ability to lead
  • Join a community of compassionate changemakers

Application Deadline

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis until:

26 May 2026

Early applicants receive priority consideration.

How to Apply

Apply here.

For more Leadership Opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post Riley’s Way Youth Leadership Retreat 2026: Fully Funded Leadership Weekend for High School Students Aged 15–18 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Third Wave Fund Mobilize Power Fund 2026: Rapid Response Grants for U.S.-Based Youth-Led and Social Justice Organizations
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Third Wave Fund Mobilize Power Fund (MPF) 2026, a rapid response grant opportunity designed to support youth-led and intergenerational organizations responding to urgent and unexpected challenges or opportunities. Administered by Third Wave Fund, the Mobilize Power Fund provides flexible funding to organizations engaged in social justice, healing justice, mutual […]

The post Third Wave Fund Mobilize Power Fund 2026: Rapid Response Grants for U.S.-Based Youth-Led and Social Justice Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Third Wave Fund Mobilize Power Fund (MPF) 2026, a rapid response grant opportunity designed to support youth-led and intergenerational organizations responding to urgent and unexpected challenges or opportunities.

Administered by Third Wave Fund, the Mobilize Power Fund provides flexible funding to organizations engaged in social justice, healing justice, mutual aid, community accountability, conflict transformation, direct action, and emergency safety and security efforts.

Since its launch in 2015, the fund has distributed more than US$4.3 million to over 500 groups, organizations, partnerships, and coalitions across the United States and U.S. territories.

About the Mobilize Power Fund

The Mobilize Power Fund supports organizations that are adapting or pivoting their work to address time-sensitive opportunities or threats that have emerged within the past six months.

The fund is intended for urgent needs rather than ongoing programming or pre-planned activities, making it particularly valuable for grassroots groups responding to rapidly changing circumstances.

What the Fund Supports

Eligible activities may include:

  • Mobilizations and direct actions
  • Healing justice work
  • Conflict resolution and conflict transformation
  • Community accountability initiatives
  • Mutual aid efforts
  • Emergency safety and security needs

Funding Benefits

Selected organizations receive rapid response grant funding to address pressing needs and strengthen their ability to respond effectively to emerging challenges and opportunities.

Who Can Apply

The fund is open to:

  • Youth-led organizations
  • Intergenerational groups
  • Nonprofit organizations
  • Coalitions and partnerships

Applicants must be based in:

  • The United States
  • U.S. territories

Organizations do not need 501(c)(3) status or fiscal sponsorship to be eligible.

Ineligible Requests

The fund does not support:

  • General operating deficits
  • Pre-planned events
  • Ongoing programming without a recent urgent trigger

Application Deadline

The first application deadline for 2026 is:

2 June 2026

How to Apply

Learn more through mpf@thirdwavefund.org.

Apply Here.

For more Grant Opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post Third Wave Fund Mobilize Power Fund 2026: Rapid Response Grants for U.S.-Based Youth-Led and Social Justice Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Policy Center for the New South Atlantic Dialogues Emerging Leaders Program 2026 in Morocco (Fully Funded)
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Key Details Program: Atlantic Dialogues Emerging Leaders (ADEL) Program 2026 Host Organization: Policy Center for the New South Location: Rabat Program Dates: December 6–12, 2026 Funding: Fully Funded Application Deadline: June 14, 2026 Eligible Regions: Africa, Europe, the Americas, and the Caribbean Age Requirement: 25–35 years old Language Requirement: English fluency Applications Open for the […]

The post Policy Center for the New South Atlantic Dialogues Emerging Leaders Program 2026 in Morocco (Fully Funded) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Key Details

  • Program: Atlantic Dialogues Emerging Leaders (ADEL) Program 2026
  • Host Organization: Policy Center for the New South
  • Location: Rabat
  • Program Dates: December 6–12, 2026
  • Funding: Fully Funded
  • Application Deadline: June 14, 2026
  • Eligible Regions: Africa, Europe, the Americas, and the Caribbean
  • Age Requirement: 25–35 years old
  • Language Requirement: English fluency

Applications Open for the Atlantic Dialogues Emerging Leaders Program 2026

Since 2012, the Atlantic Dialogues Emerging Leaders (ADEL) program has brought together over 40 emerging leaders each year, from Africa and across the Atlantic Basin, who are actively shaping policy, business, and societal debates.

Now in its 13th edition, the ADEL program has become a reference platform for connecting high-impact professionals to global conversations and long-term networks of influence.

Bringing together emerging leaders from the public sector, private sector, think tanks, academia, and civil society, the program offers a unique opportunity to engage in a space where ideas, perspectives, and decision-making intersect.

About the program

As the flagship leadership initiative of the Policy Center for the New South, the ADEL program convenes participants in Rabat, Morocco for an intensive, immersive program centered on exchange, peer engagement, and discussions on key issues shaping the Atlantic space.

Participants then join the Atlantic Dialogues conference, a platform bringing together policymakers, business leaders, and experts from across the Atlantic Basin.

Who can apply

The ADEL program is intended for professionals ready to engage in high-level, cross-sector discussions and contribute to a broader international conversation. Applicants should:

  • Be 25–35 years old (as of December 6, 2026) 

  • Be fluent in English

  • Be citizens or residents of countries in Africa or across the Atlantic Basin (Europe, the Americas, and the Caribbean)

  • Have significant professional experience in their field

  • Be engaged in roles involving responsibility, influence, or decision-making

  • Be available for the full program period (December 6–12, 2026) 

What the program offers

  • A fully funded experience in Rabat

  • Tailored leadership training and interactive workshops

  • Integration into a lifelong ADEL alumni network and community

  • Access to the Atlantic Dialogues conference

  • Direct engagement with senior decision-makers and experts

How to apply

Your application must include:

  • Resume/CV (max. 2 pages, PDF format)

  • One-Minute Private YouTube Video, answering:

    • Who are you and what’s your field of work?

    • What drives your work and its impact?

    • Why are you applying to ADEL?

    • How will you contribute to and benefit from the program?

To apply, please complete the application form no later than Sunday, June 14, 2026.

For questions, please consult our FAQs or contact us at adel2026@policycenter.ma. 

Application Form

For full details on the program and application process: ADEL 2026 Call for Applications – PDF

For more leadership development opportunities, please visit: https://opportunitiesforyouth.org/?s=leader

The post Policy Center for the New South Atlantic Dialogues Emerging Leaders Program 2026 in Morocco (Fully Funded) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Beyond Wind: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Tropical Cyclone Risk, Flooding, and Landslides at Loughborough University
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Climate-related disasters are becoming more frequent and devastating across the world, with tropical cyclones continuing to threaten millions of lives, infrastructure, economies, and ecosystems every year. While many studies focus primarily on the destructive wind speeds associated with tropical cyclones, researchers are increasingly discovering that the most severe losses often occur when multiple hazards happen […]

The post Beyond Wind: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Tropical Cyclone Risk, Flooding, and Landslides at Loughborough University appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Climate-related disasters are becoming more frequent and devastating across the world, with tropical cyclones continuing to threaten millions of lives, infrastructure, economies, and ecosystems every year. While many studies focus primarily on the destructive wind speeds associated with tropical cyclones, researchers are increasingly discovering that the most severe losses often occur when multiple hazards happen at the same time — especially flooding and landslides.

To address this critical global challenge, Loughborough University is offering an exciting fully funded PhD project titled “Beyond Wind! Where and Why Flooding and Landslides Amplify Tropical Cyclone Risk (Ref: IRISK-26-LU-03)” under its Department of Geography.

This prestigious doctoral opportunity is part of the Informatics for Multi-hazard Risk and Resilience (i-Risk) NERC Doctoral Focal Awards (DFA) and is designed for students who are passionate about climate science, disaster resilience, artificial intelligence, environmental hazards, and risk analysis.

The project combines cutting-edge AI technologies, satellite observations, hazard modelling, and disaster risk reduction strategies to improve understanding of how multiple hazards interact during tropical cyclones and how these combined hazards increase losses globally.

About the PhD Project

Tropical cyclones are among the world’s most destructive natural disasters. Although strong winds are often considered the primary threat, tropical cyclones also trigger:

  • Severe flooding
  • Coastal inundation
  • Landslides
  • Infrastructure collapse
  • Economic losses
  • Human displacement

However, scientists still lack a comprehensive understanding of how these hazards occur together and how they amplify overall disaster risks.

This PhD project seeks to fill that important knowledge gap by creating the first global and systematic record of tropical cyclone hazards involving:

  1. Wind hazards
  2. Flooding events
  3. Landslide occurrences

Using advanced satellite data and world-leading artificial intelligence tools, the research will investigate:

  • Where multiple hazards co-occur
  • Why they happen simultaneously
  • How climate patterns influence compound hazards
  • Why combined hazards produce greater destruction
  • How disaster forecasting systems can improve early warning capabilities

The project will also use detailed case studies to analyze how multiple hazards amplify losses during major tropical cyclone events.

The findings are expected to provide critical insights for:

  • Insurance industries
  • Climate adaptation planners
  • Disaster risk reduction agencies
  • Emergency response systems
  • Early warning systems
  • Infrastructure resilience planning

Research Placements and International Exposure

One of the major highlights of this PhD opportunity is the planned research placements at globally respected institutions, including:

  • British Geological Survey
  • National Center for Atmospheric Research

These placements will provide students with:

  • International research exposure
  • Access to advanced scientific tools
  • Collaboration opportunities with leading experts
  • Practical experience in hazard and climate research
  • Professional networking opportunities

Supervisory Team

The project will be supervised by experienced researchers in geography, climate science, and disaster resilience.

Primary Supervisor

  • Dr. John Hillier

Secondary Supervisor

  • Prof. Dilanthi Amaratunga

Applicants are encouraged to contact potential supervisors before submitting their applications to discuss the project and research interests.

Funding Benefits

This PhD studentship is funded by UKRI and provides extensive financial support for successful candidates.

The funding package includes:

  • Tax-free annual stipend of £21,805
  • Tuition fees covered at the UK rate
  • Duration of funding: 3.5 years
  • Potential full international fee waiver for outstanding international students
  • Research and professional development opportunities
  • International placements and collaborations

This makes the programme highly competitive and financially attractive for both UK and international applicants.

Study Duration and Mode

Applicants can choose between:

Full-Time Option

  • Duration: 3.5 years

Part-Time Option

  • Duration: 7 years

Start Date

  • October 2026

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for this PhD opportunity, applicants must possess:

  • A strong undergraduate degree or Master’s degree
  • At least a UK 2:1 honours degree or equivalent international qualification
  • Academic background in a relevant discipline

Relevant academic fields may include:

  • Civil Engineering
  • Climate Science
  • Environmental Science
  • Geography
  • Data Science
  • Hazard Modelling
  • Earth Sciences
  • Disaster Risk Management
  • AI and Informatics

International applicants must also satisfy English language requirements.

English Language Requirements

Applicants whose first language is not English are expected to demonstrate English proficiency through recognized tests such as:

  • IELTS
  • TOEFL

Minimum IELTS requirement:

  • Overall score: 6.5
  • At least 6.0 in each competency

Application Process

Applications must be submitted online through Loughborough University.

Applicants should:

  1. Select School of Social Science and Humanities under programme name
  2. Quote the project reference:
    • IRISK-26-LU-03

Required Application Documents

Candidates must upload the following documents:

1. Personal Statement (Two Pages)

The statement should include:

Section One

Research interests in:

  • Informatics
  • Disaster risk reduction
  • i-Risk DFA
  • Motivation for selecting the project
Section Two

Responses to competency questions covering:

  • Problem-solving
  • Data analytics experience
  • Goal achievement
  • Handling setbacks and troubleshooting

2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

The CV should include:

  • Academic background
  • Research interests
  • Relevant experience

3. Academic Transcripts and Certificates

Documents must be translated into English if necessary.

4. IELTS or TOEFL Certificate

Required for applicable international applicants.

Selection Criteria

Applications will be assessed based on:

  • Motivation and career aspirations
  • Intellectual excellence and academic potential
  • Suitability for the project
  • Alignment with i-Risk objectives

Why This PhD Opportunity Matters

This project addresses one of the world’s most urgent climate and disaster challenges.

As climate change intensifies extreme weather events, understanding compound hazards becomes increasingly important for protecting communities and infrastructure.

The successful candidate will contribute to:

  • Global disaster resilience research
  • Climate adaptation strategies
  • AI-driven hazard analysis
  • Improved early warning systems
  • Future climate risk policies

This is an exceptional opportunity for students interested in combining technology, climate science, and disaster resilience to create real-world impact.

Important Deadline

Application Deadline: 9 June 2026

Interested candidates are encouraged to prepare their applications early due to the competitive nature of the studentship.

CLICK TO APPLY

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global PhD Scholarship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Beyond Wind: Fully Funded PhD Opportunity in Tropical Cyclone Risk, Flooding, and Landslides at Loughborough University appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Manus Fellow Program 2026: Join a Global Network Shaping the Future of AI Agents
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The future of artificial intelligence is rapidly evolving, and the Manus Fellow Program 2026 is creating an opportunity for innovators, entrepreneurs, AI experts, and community builders worldwide to take part in that transformation. Designed for AI-native thinkers and industry leaders, the Manus Fellowship is a hands-on immersive program that empowers professionals to explore the frontier […]

The post Manus Fellow Program 2026: Join a Global Network Shaping the Future of AI Agents appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The future of artificial intelligence is rapidly evolving, and the Manus Fellow Program 2026 is creating an opportunity for innovators, entrepreneurs, AI experts, and community builders worldwide to take part in that transformation.

Designed for AI-native thinkers and industry leaders, the Manus Fellowship is a hands-on immersive program that empowers professionals to explore the frontier of general AI agents while helping organizations and communities adopt AI-powered workflows and automation.

From hosting professional events to mentoring business teams and demonstrating real-world AI automation impact, Manus Fellows are becoming global voices driving the next generation of intelligent systems.

If you are passionate about AI, productivity, innovation, entrepreneurship, or business transformation, this fellowship offers a unique opportunity to collaborate with a growing international network of forward-thinking professionals.

What is the Manus Fellow Program?

The Manus Fellow Program is a global fellowship initiative focused on helping professionals and innovators shape the future of general AI agents.

The program brings together:

  • AI enthusiasts
  • Industry experts
  • Startup founders
  • Builders and hackers
  • Community leaders
  • Consultants
  • Students
  • Product growth specialists
  • Automation advocates

Through the fellowship, selected fellows work closely with the Manus team to explore advanced AI-driven workflows and demonstrate how AI agents can improve productivity, operations, and business performance.

The program encourages fellows to become ambassadors of AI-powered transformation within their communities and industries.

Mission of the Manus Fellowship

The Manus Fellowship aims to:

  1. Accelerate AI adoption globally
  2. Build strong AI-focused communities
  3. Showcase real-world AI automation use cases
  4. Encourage innovation in agent-based workflows
  5. Help shape the future development of Manus products

The fellowship supports professionals who are actively experimenting with AI tools and applying them in practical business and operational environments.

Who Should Apply for the Manus Fellowship?

The Manus Fellow Program is looking for individuals who are actively contributing to the AI ecosystem and helping others understand the power of intelligent automation.

Ideal applicants include:

1. Local Community Builders and KOLs

The program seeks community organizers and key opinion leaders across:

  • Artificial intelligence
  • Entrepreneurship
  • Productivity
  • Technology
  • Creator economy
  • Innovation ecosystems

Applicants who host events, build communities, or educate audiences about AI technologies are highly encouraged to apply.

2. Industry Experts

Professionals with experience implementing AI solutions in real business settings can play an important role in the fellowship.

This includes individuals who can:

  • Demonstrate AI ROI
  • Improve business workflows
  • Automate operations
  • Lead digital transformation projects
  • Train teams on AI adoption

3. Manus Power Users

The fellowship welcomes advanced users who push AI tools to their limits and actively explore what intelligent agents can accomplish.

These applicants may already be:

  • Testing complex workflows
  • Building AI systems
  • Creating automation frameworks
  • Experimenting with AI integrations
  • Developing productivity systems

4. AI-First Innovators

The program is also ideal for innovators building AI-native systems, workflows, and organizations.

This may include:

  • Startup founders
  • Product builders
  • AI consultants
  • Operations specialists
  • Researchers
  • Workflow architects

Benefits of the Manus Fellow Program

Selected Manus Fellows receive access to multiple benefits designed to help them grow their influence, expand their AI expertise, and strengthen their communities.

Fellowship Benefits Include:

Event Support and Community Funding

Fellows receive:

  • Event stipends
  • Community initiative support
  • Official Manus swag
  • Resources for local activations

This enables fellows to organize impactful events and educational sessions within their regions.

Free Manus Credits

Participants gain free access to Manus credits throughout the fellowship duration, allowing them to:

  • Explore advanced workflows
  • Test AI automation systems
  • Build experimental use cases
  • Create scalable AI-powered processes

Direct Access to the Manus Team

Fellows receive a direct communication channel with the Manus team, providing opportunities for:

  • Product feedback
  • Collaboration
  • Networking
  • Insights into upcoming developments

Early Access to Product Releases

Selected fellows may receive early access to new Manus features and releases before they become publicly available.

Visibility and Recognition

The fellowship provides exposure through:

  • Community shoutouts
  • Global recognition
  • Featured opportunities
  • Speaking engagements
  • Event hosting opportunities

This visibility can significantly strengthen a fellow’s professional profile in the AI ecosystem.

Responsibilities of Manus Fellows

During the fellowship, fellows are expected to contribute actively to the growth and adoption of AI-powered systems.

Responsibilities may include:

  • Translating complex workflows into AI-agent solutions
  • Hosting community events and activations
  • Demonstrating AI implementation strategies
  • Sharing product feedback
  • Educating businesses and communities about AI automation
  • Building local or digital AI communities

Global Fellowship Network

The Manus Fellowship Network is rapidly expanding across major global cities and innovation hubs.

Cities and Regions Mentioned Include:

  • New York
  • San Francisco
  • Paris
  • London
  • Tokyo
  • Seoul
  • Singapore
  • Riyadh

The program also supports participation across:

  • Entrepreneurship hubs
  • Consulting ecosystems
  • Retail industries
  • Tech communities
  • Creator economies
  • Student innovation networks

Meet the Manus Fellows

The fellowship has already attracted a diverse and impressive group of innovators from around the world.

Some featured fellows include:

  • Abhishek Suryawanshi – AI and open knowledge advocate from San Francisco
  • Ahmed Ezzat – AI engineer from Cairo
  • Brandon Chen – Entrepreneur and community builder from Berkeley
  • Cho Chan Myei Oo – Product growth and partnership expert from Singapore
  • Danilo Brizola – CEO and AI enabler from Brazil
  • Eva Wedra Kačková – Founder and AI explorer from Slovakia
  • Gary Liew – Entrepreneurship and tech-focused MBA student from London
  • Glen Gao – Builder with experience at Meta and HRT
  • Grant McNaughton – Founder and entrepreneur from San Francisco

These fellows represent the diversity and global reach of the Manus community.

Why the Manus Fellow Program Matters

Artificial intelligence is changing how businesses operate, how teams collaborate, and how productivity systems are designed.

Programs like the Manus Fellowship are important because they:

  • Help democratize access to AI innovation
  • Empower local AI ecosystems
  • Encourage responsible AI experimentation
  • Promote collaboration between industries and communities
  • Accelerate practical AI adoption worldwide

As organizations increasingly seek AI-driven efficiency and automation, professionals who understand intelligent workflows will become even more valuable.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply directly through the Manus Fellowship application portal.

Applicants should prepare to showcase:

  • Their AI experience
  • Community involvement
  • Innovation projects
  • Business transformation initiatives
  • AI workflow experimentation
  • Leadership in AI-related spaces

Deadline

No official application deadline has been stated.

Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Manus Fellow Program 2026: Join a Global Network Shaping the Future of AI Agents appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded PhD at Loughborough University 2026 in Multi-Hazard Early Warning Systems and Disaster Resilience (IRISK-26-LU-02)
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
Applications are now open for a fully funded PhD opportunity at Loughborough University focusing on the technical, legal, and socio-cultural complexities involved in communicating multi-hazard early warning systems to jurisdictional agencies and emergency response partners. Hosted within the School of Architecture, Building and Civil Engineering, this doctoral research project forms part of the prestigious Informatics […]

The post Fully Funded PhD at Loughborough University 2026 in Multi-Hazard Early Warning Systems and Disaster Resilience (IRISK-26-LU-02) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for a fully funded PhD opportunity at Loughborough University focusing on the technical, legal, and socio-cultural complexities involved in communicating multi-hazard early warning systems to jurisdictional agencies and emergency response partners.

Hosted within the School of Architecture, Building and Civil Engineering, this doctoral research project forms part of the prestigious Informatics for Multi-hazard Risk and Resilience (i-Risk) NERC Doctoral Focal Awards in Environmental Sciences.

The project is supervised by Prof Dilanthi Amaratunga and Dr John Hillier and offers an outstanding opportunity for researchers interested in disaster risk reduction, climate resilience, governance systems, informatics, civil engineering, and emergency management.

With global climate risks intensifying and disaster events becoming increasingly complex, the need for effective Multi-Hazard Early Warning Systems (MHEWS) has become a major international priority. This PhD project aims to investigate one of the most overlooked areas within disaster governance: the interface between hazard detection systems and operational emergency response agencies.

About the PhD Project

The research project was developed in response to the devastating coastal floods that affected South Asia in late 2025, highlighting urgent global weaknesses in disaster preparedness and emergency communication systems.

Although governance structures are widely recognized as essential to sustainable disaster management systems, the transition point between upstream hazard monitoring systems and downstream emergency response operations remains poorly understood.

This “interface” is often characterized by:

  • Jurisdictional complexity
  • Institutional fragmentation
  • Communication barriers
  • Legal coordination challenges
  • Technical interoperability issues
  • Socio-cultural response dynamics

The PhD research seeks to examine these governance arrangements in detail and identify ways to strengthen coordination between:

  • Regional information providers
  • Hazard detection systems
  • National agencies
  • Sub-national emergency operations centers
  • Disaster response stakeholders

Ultimately, the project aims to contribute toward long-term disaster resilience and improved multi-hazard preparedness frameworks globally.

Research Areas and Academic Focus

This interdisciplinary PhD combines multiple fields including:

  • Civil Engineering
  • Climate Science
  • Politics and Government
  • Disaster Risk Reduction
  • Environmental Informatics
  • Governance and Public Policy
  • Emergency Management
  • Resilience Studies

The project is particularly suitable for candidates interested in understanding how technology, governance, and human systems interact during disaster response and resilience planning.

Funding Information

This is a fully funded UKRI studentship covering 3.5 years of doctoral study.

Funding includes:

  • A tax-free stipend of £21,805 per year
  • Tuition fees at the UK rate
  • Potential full international tuition fee waiver for outstanding international applicants

The programme notes that, due to UKRI funding regulations, no more than 30% of funded studentships can be awarded to international students.

Funding Duration

  • Full-time: 3.5 years
  • Part-time: 7 years

Start Date

  • October 2026

Bench Fees

  • No bench fees required

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Hold, or expect to obtain shortly, a strong undergraduate or Master’s degree
  • Have at least a UK 2:1 honours degree or equivalent international qualification
  • Graduate from a recognized high-ranking university
  • Demonstrate strong academic and research potential

Relevant academic backgrounds may include:

  • Civil Engineering
  • Climate Science
  • Environmental Science
  • Disaster Risk Management
  • Geography
  • Political Science
  • Public Policy
  • Data Science
  • Informatics
  • Governance Studies
  • Related interdisciplinary fields

English Language Requirements

International applicants must meet the university’s English language requirements.

Applicants are expected to achieve:

  • IELTS score of 6.5 overall
  • Minimum 6.0 in each competency

Equivalent TOEFL or recognized English proficiency qualifications may also be accepted.

What Applicants Must Submit

During the online application process, candidates must upload:

Personal Statement

A two-page personal statement divided into two sections:

Section One

One page discussing:

  • Research interests in informatics and disaster risk reduction
  • Interest in the i-Risk Doctoral Focal Award
  • Rationale for selecting the project

Section Two

Responses to four reflective questions addressing:

  • Problem-solving and innovation
  • Data analytics experience
  • Goal-setting and motivation
  • Handling setbacks and troubleshooting

Additional Documents

Applicants must also provide:

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • Academic transcripts
  • Degree certificates
  • IELTS or TOEFL certificate (if applicable)

Application Assessment Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on:

  • Motivation and career aspirations
  • Intellectual excellence and research potential
  • Suitability for the specific project
  • Alignment with the i-Risk programme

Interview Process

Shortlisted candidates are expected to attend remote interviews via Microsoft Teams during the week commencing 29 June 2026.

Applicants are also encouraged to contact supervisors before applying to discuss project-specific questions.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted online through Loughborough University.

Applicants should:

  • Select “Architecture, Building and Civil Engineering” under programme name
  • Quote the reference: IRISK-26-LU-02

Important Contacts

  • General enquiries: irisk@mailbox.lboro.ac.uk
  • School contact: abce.pgr@mailbox.lboro.ac.uk

Why Study at Loughborough University?

Loughborough University is internationally recognized for excellence in teaching, research, and postgraduate education.

The university has received recognition through the FindAMasters and FindAPhD Postgrad Awards and is known for:

  • High-quality research supervision
  • Strong doctoral training environments
  • International academic reputation
  • Supportive postgraduate community
  • Interdisciplinary research opportunities

Doctoral researchers at Loughborough gain opportunities to contribute new knowledge to globally significant challenges while developing advanced academic, analytical, and professional skills.

Application Deadline

📅 Closing Date: 9 June 2026

Interested applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their materials early due to the competitive nature of UKRI-funded doctoral positions.

Final Thoughts

This fully funded PhD at Loughborough University offers a rare opportunity to conduct impactful interdisciplinary research at the intersection of disaster governance, climate resilience, informatics, and emergency response systems.

For students passionate about disaster risk reduction, policy coordination, climate resilience, and sustainable governance systems, this project provides not only financial support but also access to a world-class research environment tackling some of the most pressing global challenges of our time.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Fully Funded PhD at Loughborough University 2026 in Multi-Hazard Early Warning Systems and Disaster Resilience (IRISK-26-LU-02) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
